main_sections.title: The Old Stag
main_sections.prose_summary: An old stag, beloved by all animals and birds in a lush forest, lives on a hillock with tender grass. When he falls ill, his friends visit to check on him. However, while wishing him speedy recovery, the animals greedily eat the tender grass on his hillock. Word spreads and more visitors come and feast on the grass. The stag grows weaker as the grass diminishes, and his friends feel ashamed when they realise that their greed has deprived their sick friend of his only food. They decide to stop eating the hillock's grass and some bring fresh leaves for the stag. After good rains, the grass grows back, the stag recovers, and all the animals learn the lesson: 'Nature has enough for everybody. But we need to use its resources wisely.' Adapted from The Panchatantra.

reading.section_name: Let us Read + Let us Think A

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Read: Read the prose story 'The Old Stag' (pp. 43–46). New Words listed: lush, stag, hillock, tender, sheltering, recovery.

Answer the following.
1. Why was the old stag popular?
2. Why did the old stag not go down the hillock to meet his friends?
3. Why did the green cover of the hillock slowly vanish?
4. Did the animals realise their mistake towards the end? What did they do?

question_bank.stem: Why was the old stag popular?
Why did the old stag not go down the hillock to meet his friends?
Why did the green cover of the hillock slowly vanish?
Did the animals realise their mistake towards the end? What did they do?

oracy.section_name: Let us Think B + Let us Speak

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Think and Discuss.
1. Did the animals do the right thing in the end? Why do you think so?
2. What would happen to the green plants around us if each one of us thought only of our own needs?

question_bank.stem: Did the animals do the right thing in the end? Why do you think so?
What would happen to the green plants around us if each one of us thought only of our own needs?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Look at this picture. Imagine a large bowl of delicious jalebis is in your classroom. You and your friends start enjoying the sweets, one by one. Everyone loves the jalebis so much that you keep reaching for more. Now, think about what might happen next. Discuss the following in the class:
1. How would you feel if the jalebis were almost finished, but some friends hadn't had enough yet?
2. What will happen to the bowl of jelebis after some time?
3. Why is it important to share when we have something that everyone enjoys?
4. How can we make sure there are enough jalebis for everyone to enjoy?

question_bank.stem: How would you feel if the jalebis were almost finished, but some friends hadn't had enough yet?
What will happen to the bowl of jelebis after some time?
Why is it important to share when we have something that everyone enjoys?
How can we make sure there are enough jalebis for everyone to enjoy?

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Four children sitting at a table with a large bowl of jalebis; one child is in a wheelchair

word_work.section_name: Let us Learn A + Let us Learn B + Let us Learn C

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Match the words to their meaning.
1. Lush forest — cool resting place
2. Tender grass — green and dense woods/forest
3. Shaded spot — quick healing
4. Healthy food — delicate young grass
5. Speedy recovery — nourishing wholesome food

question_bank.stem: Match: Lush forest / Tender grass / Shaded spot / Healthy food / Speedy recovery → cool resting place / green and dense woods/forest / quick healing / delicate young grass / nourishing wholesome food

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Look at the following words: lush forest, friendly animals, shaded spot, tender grass, healthy food. These words are descriptive words that give us more details about scenes, people and objects. These words are used to make writing more interesting. Draw the image that comes to your mind when you read the following: lush forest / tender grass / healthy food.

question_bank.stem: Draw the image that comes to your mind when you read 'lush forest'.
Draw the image that comes to your mind when you read 'tender grass'.
Draw the image that comes to your mind when you read 'healthy food'.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following sentence: 'Each day, he warmly greeted his fellow animals and birds.' The word 'warmly' is an adverb. Here, 'warmly' tells the way or the manner of the stag's greeting. Examples: The old stag slowly recovered. / The rabbit quickly ate the carrots. Encircle the adverbs that describe how something is done from the table: soon, badly, lately, cheerfully, quietly, early, carefully, highly, fast, regularly, nearby, loudly. Now work with your partner and write a sentence for each encircled adverb.

question_bank.stem: Encircle the adverbs that describe how something is done from the table: soon, badly, lately, cheerfully, quietly, early, carefully, highly, fast, regularly, nearby, loudly.
Now work with your partner and write a sentence for each encircled adverb.

writing.section_name: Let us Write A + Let us Write B

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Complete the story by writing dialogues in the speech blurbs. The Monkeys' Fast — a picture-comic story showing monkey king declaring a fast; other monkeys agreeing, then collecting bananas, tempted to eat, finally breaking the fast and concluding 'Well... maybe fasting takes more self-control than we thought!'

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Six-panel picture comic showing monkeys declaring a fast, collecting bananas, being tempted, and finally breaking the fast

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Write about your best friend and complete the paragraph. My best friend's name is ___. ___ is a ___. ___ is ___ years old. ___ studies in class ___. There are ___ members in ___ family. ___ hobbies are ___. ___ wants to be a ___. When we are together, we ___. ___ is my best friend because ___.

Create a Thank You Card from the Old Stag. Instructions: Imagine you are the old stag, grateful to the friends who helped you while you were sick. Prepare a thank you card to show your appreciation. Front of the Card: Draw a picture of the stag surrounded by his friends in the forest. Inside the Card: Write a kind thank you message to his friends, expressing how their help made him feel better.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Illustration of a thank-you card with the old stag surrounded by forest animals
